title: styrax crotonoides subsp. fraserensis
intro: Styrax crotonoides subsp. fraserensis, also known as Fraser's styrax or Fraser's benzoin, is a plant species within the family Styracaceae. It is a tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia, known for its fragrant resin, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The resin of Styrax crotonoides subsp. fraserensis contains compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wound healing, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced skin health.
